title: Long-Term Legal Status for Ukrainians on Temporary Protection - Poland
title_local: Status pobytowy dla obywateli Ukrainy - ochrona czasowa i dalsze opcje
summary: Ukrainians with temporary protection in Poland may have several legal pathways for longer-term stay depending on residence history, work status, and individual circumstances.
steps: Confirm your temporary protection and PESEL UKR status are active.
If missing PESEL, register at your local municipality office.
Track official extension announcements for temporary protection validity.
Evaluate temporary residence permit route if you meet work or family criteria.
Submit residence application to the Voivodeship Office with complete documents.
Evaluate international protection or other status options if relevant to your case.
Maintain address registration and keep status documents up to date.
documents: Ukrainian passport or other identity document
For residence permit: work or family supporting documentation
office: Municipality office for PESEL and address matters; Voivodeship Office for residence permit applications.
fee: Temporary protection registration is free; residence permit fees depend on permit type
notes: Temporary protection timelines are policy-driven and can be extended or adjusted by legislation.
